category_title: Students with EBD exhibiting a personal reward moral orientation and improved classroom compliance
scenario_title: Perspective taking during hypothetical moral dilemmas
minimal_context: Violet is participating in an individual interview session where she is asked to read hypothetical moral dilemmas and take the perspective of the characters.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at this story. Judy saved up her money to go to a concert, but her mother told her she has to use the money to buy school clothes instead. What do you think the mother should do?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can answer that! If I was the mother, yes, I would have told her no because she probably paid for Judy’s school clothes every year and she gonna be getting tired of that.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Why do you think the mother would be tired of paying for the clothes?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: She probably want to buy her[self] some new outfits or something.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's try to take Judy's perspective now. How do you think Judy feels about missing the concert?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Probably not for her, but for me, yeah. If I was Judy, I wouldn't care about the concert. I would just want to keep the money to buy myself some new outfits or something.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at this story. Judy saved up her money to go to a concert, but her mother told her she has to use the money to buy school clothes instead. What do you think the mother should do?

STUDENT: I can answer that! If I was the mother, yes, I would have told her no because she probably paid for Judy’s school clothes every year and she gonna be getting tired of that.

TUTOR: Why do you think the mother would be tired of paying for the clothes?

STUDENT: She probably want to buy her[self] some new outfits or something.

TUTOR: Let's try to take Judy's perspective now. How do you think Judy feels about missing the concert?

STUDENT: Probably not for her, but for me, yeah. If I was Judy, I wouldn't care about the concert. I would just want to keep the money to buy myself some new outfits or something.

task.content: Engaged in perspective taking during hypothetical moral dilemmas such as The Long Walk, Louise's Dilemma, and The Heinz Dilemma
task.learning_goal: Reasoning about the effectiveness of punishment versus explanation and attempting to take the perspective of characters

context.social_setting: Interacting with the researcher during individual interview sessions

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When she engaged in perspective taking
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Attempts that usually amounted to nothing more than a projection of her own wants and desires onto another
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Calculating the liabilities and projecting self-interest
related_quotes: If I was the mother, yes, I would have told her no because she probably paid for Judy’s school clothes every year and she gonna be getting tired of that. She probably want to buy her[self] some new outfits or something.

That doesn’t make no sense because even if he do get the medicine and give it to his wife, he’s still not going to get away with that. They gonna catch him eventually. . . If his wife do get unsick or she do get well, he’s not gonna be able to see her.

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to decide how to fairly divide one lunch between two children when one child is younger than the other
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Assumed the role of the older child and said that she would split the lunch evenly with the younger one
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Egocentrism serving as an adaptive response
related_quotes: Probably not for her, but for me, yeah.

behavior_patterns.condition: When reasoning about the effectiveness of punishment versus explanation
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Concluded that a parent explaining the impropriety of his actions to a child would do no good. The child would do it again unless he was punished.
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Personal reward moral orientation prioritizing affiliation and avoiding trouble
related_quotes: Your father won’t whip you if he don’t love you, but like he do whip you and then he love you anyways.
Well he should say the boy didn’t do anything cause that’s his brother and he might do something like his brother did and he wouldn’t want nobody to tell on him, what he did.
